title: Payback Period Rule: If a project costs 200000 and generates cash inflows of...
summary: Calculate the payback period by dividing the initial investment by annual cash inflows.
prompt: If a project costs $200,000 and generates cash inflows of $40,000 for 5 years, what is the payback period?
solution: Payback Period = Initial Investment / Annual Cash Flow. Thus, $200,000 / $40,000 = 5 years.
